layout_description:
Option: OPT-1-REVISED
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE LAYOUT (REVISED FOR FIT). The slide presents a comprehensive project workplan as a single unified table spanning the full usable slide area. The table is structured as a 5-column grid (Workstream, Details, Owner(s), and three timeline columns: 2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, and 2027) with five major workstream rows. The layout uses a flat geometry table design (Section 4 Option C) appropriate for formal project documentation. All content is contained within a single table structure with no outer container, maximizing space for the dense tabular content. The table fills the slide from edge to edge within safe margins, with minimal padding to accommodate the high information density. Dashed separator lines between rows provide visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. The timeline header row uses a light brand-colored fill to distinguish deliverable periods from the workstream structure. CRITICAL REVISION: Timeline column widths have been increased from 47% to 55% total width to eliminate text overlap. Row heights have been increased and font size reduced to 9-10pt to ensure all multi-line deliverable content fits without stacking or overlap. Column width redistribution: Workstream reduced to 12%, Details reduced to 18%, Owner(s) reduced to 15%, timeline columns expanded to 55% total (16% + 13% + 13% + 13%).

Blueprint:
  Orientation: grid
  Regions: 6
  Allocation: [0.08, 0.184, 0.184, 0.184, 0.184, 0.184]

Containers:
  C0 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE): A comprehensive project workplan table built using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) principles, adapted for a 5-column × 6-row structure (1 header row + 5 workstream rows). The table spans the full usable slide width and height, filling all available space below the title with minimal top margin.

COLUMN STRUCTURE (left to right, REVISED WIDTHS):
1. Workstream column (12% width, REDUCED from 15%): Contains workstream identifiers (i. Conduct discovery, ii. Select and onboard partner, iii. Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC, iv. Implement fund accounting platform - Holdings, v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments). Each workstream name is bold, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset. Light grey fill for all workstream cells to distinguish them as row labels.

2. Details column (18% width, REDUCED from 20%): Contains activity descriptions for each workstream. Multiple detail items per workstream row, each as a separate text line. Body font, regular weight, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset. White background. Font size: 9-10pt to maintain uniformity with deliverable cells.

3. Owner(s) column (15% width, REDUCED from 18%): Lists responsible parties with role prefixes (Finance/Ops:, Tech:, TO:). Role prefixes in regular weight, names in regular weight, brand text color. Multiple owners per row separated by line breaks. Left-aligned with small left inset. White background. Font size: 9-10pt to maintain uniformity.

4-7. Timeline columns (55% width total, INCREASED from 47%, distributed as: 2026 Q2 = 16%, Q3 = 13%, Q4 = 13%, 2027 = 13%): Deliverables against each workstream for each time period. Each cell contains bullet points (brand-color circle bullets, small size) with deliverable text in body font, left-aligned with small left inset. White background for all deliverable cells. Font size: 9-10pt maximum to fit dense multi-line content without overlap. Line height: 1.3-1.4× font size to ensure bullet points render on separate lines with proper vertical spacing.

HEADER ROW TREATMENT:
- Column headers (Workstream, Details, Owner(s)) are bold, brand text color, left-aligned, white background, no filled header bar.
- Below each of these three headers: a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned beneath the header text, sitting fully below the text without overlap).
- Below each accent tab: a thin solid horizontal line (brand primary color) spanning that column's width only (not crossing into adjacent columns — small white gutters between columns).
- Timeline header row (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) spans the four rightmost columns with a unified light brand-colored fill (light tint of brand primary), white bold text centered horizontally within each period cell, vertically centered. This header row has no accent tabs — the filled background distinguishes it. A thin solid horizontal line (brand primary color) runs along the bottom edge of the timeline header row, spanning all four timeline columns.
- The text "Deliverables against each workstream" appears above the timeline header cells, centered across all four timeline columns, in a smaller font size (8-9pt), brand text color, regular weight.

ROW STRUCTURE (REVISED FOR FIT):
- Five workstream rows distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow values.
- CRITICAL: Each row's height is INCREASED to provide sufficient vertical space for multi-line deliverable content. Rows must be tall enough that 3-4 bullet points with wrapped text can render without overlap. Minimum row height target: each row should accommodate at least 4 lines of 9-10pt text with 1.3-1.4× line height spacing.
- Within each row, all cells across the five columns share identical top and bottom edges (horizontal alignment rule B2).
- Content within each cell is top-aligned with a small top inset, allowing multi-line text to flow naturally downward.
- VERIFICATION REQUIREMENT: All deliverable content from the source slide must be placed in the correct column AND correct row — no transposition of cells between columns or rows. Each bullet point must render on its own line with proper vertical spacing so text does not stack on top of itself.

ROW SEPARATORS:
- Thin dashed grey horizontal lines between consecutive workstream rows (not after the last row).
- Separators span the full table width from left edge to right edge.
- Positioned at the boundary between rows, sitting tight to the content (minimal space above and below on this dense slide).

FONT SIZING (REVISED FOR FIT):
- All body text (details, owners, deliverables) uses the same font size throughout the table to maintain within-slide uniformity (Rule C6).
- Font size target: 9-10pt maximum, optimized to fit the densest cells (deliverable cells with 3-4 multi-line bullet points) without overflow or text stacking.
- Line height for all body text: 1.3-1.4× font size to ensure proper vertical spacing between lines and bullet points.
- Column headers: same size as body text (9-10pt) or slightly larger (10-11pt), bold weight for hierarchy.
- Workstream labels: same size as body text (9-10pt), bold weight.
- Timeline header text (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027): 10-11pt bold, white color.
- "Deliverables against each workstream" label: 8-9pt regular weight.

VISUAL TREATMENT:
- No outer container border or rounded rectangle — the table sits directly on the slide background.
- No cell borders except the header underlines and row separators described above.
- Light grey fill for the Workstream column (all cells) to visually distinguish row labels from content.
- White fill for all other cells.
- No drop shadows, glows, or 3D effects.
- Minimal padding within cells (small insets on all sides, approximately 2-3% of cell width/height) to maximize space for content on this dense slide while preventing text from touching cell edges.

CONTENT DENSITY HANDLING (REVISED APPROACH):
- This slide has very high content density (5 workstreams × 3 descriptive columns × 4 timeline columns = 35 content cells, many with multi-line text and bullet lists).
- Padding is minimized to the smallest acceptable value that prevents text from touching cell edges (Rule 0.1 — dense content requires tight padding).
- Dashed separator lines provide visual rhythm and section boundaries without consuming vertical space (Rule 0.10).
- Font size is reduced to 9-10pt maximum and applied uniformly across all body text to maintain professional consistency and fit the densest cells without overflow (Rule C6).
- Timeline column widths have been increased to 55% total (from 47%) to provide adequate horizontal space for multi-line deliverable text.
- Row heights have been increased to provide adequate vertical space for 3-4 bullet points per cell with proper line spacing.
- The table fills the full available slide height, distributed evenly across the five workstream rows, ensuring no large empty zone appears at the bottom (Rule 0.3 — vertical rhythm).
- CRITICAL FIT VERIFICATION: Each deliverable cell must render all bullet points sequentially (one per line) with proper vertical spacing (line-height = 1.3-1.4× font size) so text does not overlap or stack. If any cell content still overflows at 10pt, reduce to 9pt uniformly across all body text.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide content (full-width workplan table) fills the available space completely. No decorative background layer is needed — adding one would violate Rule D1 (bottom layer used only when content does not fill the slide). The table's light grey fill in the Workstream column and the timeline header's light brand fill provide sufficient visual structure without requiring an additional background layer.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. This slide contains a single unified table structure with no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. All visual relationships (workstream → details → owners → timeline deliverables) are expressed through the table's row structure and do not require top-layer connector elements.

_reference_violations.description: Severe text overlap and stacking within deliverable cells (timeline columns). In the 2026 Q2 column, multiple rows show text rendering on top of itself with no vertical spacing between bullet points. Text appears as dense black blocks rather than separate lines. This is visible in rows i, ii, and iii where deliverable content is illegible due to overlapping text lines.

Cell content transposition detected. Comparing the sketch to the source slide, several deliverable items appear in the wrong timeline column. For example, in row ii (Select and onboard partner), the Q3 column in the sketch shows content that should appear in Q4, and Q4 content appears shifted. The column/row placement of specific deliverables does not match the source slide's structure.

Timeline columns (Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) are too narrow for the multi-line deliverable content. Despite the layout description specifying 55% total width for timeline columns, the rendered cells show severe text overflow and stacking, indicating insufficient horizontal and/or vertical space. Font size appears too large (likely >10pt) for the available cell dimensions, causing text to render on top of itself rather than wrapping to new lines with proper spacing.

Row v (Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments) appears truncated or cut off at the bottom of the slide. The source slide shows this row with full content, but in the sketch the bottom portion of row v is not fully visible, suggesting the table height exceeds the available slide area or row heights are not properly distributed.
